We take what the land leaves behind — old barn wood, weathered iron, salvaged cedar — and build pieces that belong in your home. Every grain, every patina, every mark tells the story.

Built with Love
Jennifer & Patrick
New Braunfels, TX
“Every piece carries the hands that made it.”
Jennifer Flint

Built by Two.
From the Heart of It.

We’re Jennifer and Patrick Flint. From a workshop in the Texas Hill Country, we build things that carry a story before we ever touch them — reclaimed barn wood, salvaged iron, weathered cedar that has survived Texas summers and decades of living.

Jennifer brings the eye — the botanical instinct, the color sense, the feeling that a piece should have when it finds its place in a home. Patrick brings the iron — the craft, the structure, the satisfaction of a joint that fits perfectly.

Together, we bring the story. And we believe that’s worth something in a world full of mass-produced everything.

Jennifer & Patrick
New Braunfels, Texas · Hill Country Made
Handcrafted Goods

The Collection

Every piece is built to order or pulled from limited workshop stock. No two are exactly alike.

Commission Work

Something Uniquely Yours

Tell us your vision. We’ll build it from the wood up.

Start Your Custom Order

No commitment yet — describe what you’re imagining and we’ll respond within 24 hours.

How It Works

  • Submit your idea — no commitment yet
  • We respond within 24 hours with a quote
  • 50% deposit holds your place in our queue
  • Build photos shared as work progresses
  • Final approval before shipping or pickup
  • Balance due on completion

Popular Requests

  • Family name flag for porch or ranch gate
  • Personalized cutting board for weddings
  • Military branch flag with service member’s name
  • Ranch brand ironwork sign
  • Live-edge wood bowl
  • Custom address sign with ironwork numbers

Turnaround

  • Standard custom: 2–4 weeks
  • Rush available — call to arrange
  • Holiday: order by November 15
Texas Hill Country

The Guide

We live here. This is our honest guide — not a sponsored list.

“The Hill Country isn’t a destination. It’s a state of mind — cedar-scented air, limestone bluffs, cold spring-fed rivers, and a pace that reminds you what living is actually for.”

🎣
Fishing
Best Holes on the Guadalupe & Comal
Rainbow trout, bass, and perch. We’ll tell you exactly where to stand.
🪞
Hiking
Trails Worth the Sweat
Enchanted Rock to quiet cedar-lined paths above Wimberley.
🍽
Food & Drink
Eat Like a Local
BBQ joints, taco trucks, the Gruene dance hall burger. Real picks.
⛺
Camping
Camp Under Texas Stars
Primitive spots on the Guadalupe, Garner State Park, Pedernales Falls.
🌿
Gardening
What Grows Here
Bluebonnets, Texas sage, pomegranate, fig. Jennifer’s plant diary.
🍆
Hidden Gems
Places Tourists Don’t Know
The swimming hole past the cattle guard. The antique barn off FM 306.

Hill Country Guide Services

Want a local to show you around? Not a tour company — a couple who loves where they live.

Request a Day Guide
  • Fishing Half-Day
  • Wildflower Hike
  • Antique Hunt Tour
  • Craft Brewery Run
  • Canyon Lake Sunset
  • Open Studio Visit
  • Couples Day Out
  • Custom Experience

What People Say

★★★★★
“Patrick built us a custom American flag with my husband’s Army unit crest burned into the canton. I cried when I opened it. This isn’t a product — it’s art with real meaning.”

San Antonio, TX
★★★★★
“Found them at Gruene Market and bought a birdhouse on the spot. Went home, ordered raised beds and a Texas flag. Quality beyond anything you’ll find in a store.”

“The personalized cutting board was absolutely stunning. Jennifer communicated every step. It became the most commented-on wedding gift they received.”
